Having Phones at School: A Student’s Perspective

I’ve always been a bit of a tech enthusiast, so when I hear people debate whether phones should be allowed at school, I have a few thoughts. On one hand, phones can be super helpful for staying connected and keeping up with homework. But on the other hand, I’ve seen firsthand how they can turn into a major distraction. So, I think it’s important to figure out a balance.

Having Phones at School: A Casual Take

Most folks would love having their phones during their free time, but some totally wouldn't for sure. I mean, yeah, having phones at school could be a cool idea, but not during class hours. If we had cell phones in class, no one would learn anything because they'd be texting instead of listening. If no one paid attention, they'd end up with no good job and a lousy education.

Having Phones at School

Most people enjoy having cell phones on their free time, though some don’t. I think allowing phones at school could have its benefits, but not during class hours. If phones were allowed in class, students might lose focus, as they’d likely send texts instead of paying attention. This could lead to poor learning outcomes, making it harder for students to secure good jobs later on.

**Dear Principal,**

Hi Principal,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to talk to you about a rule that's been bothering me. I don't think it's fair that students who get C averages can't play in sports. It seems like a tough call to me because, well, I've been in that situation before.

When I was in school, getting Cs felt like a struggle. I remember putting in the effort, staying after class, and still not meeting the standards. But despite that, I was part of the basketball and track teams.

Dear Principal,

I don’t think it’s fair that kids who get C’s can’t play sports. Like, why not? In my opinion, they should still be allowed to. I mean, I’ve dealt with C’s myself, and I’ve been on the basketball and track teams. We work our butts off just to get those C’s, right? If you take that away, I don’t think many kids would wanna be athletes. Why? Because it’s super hard to just scrape by with a B or an A. So, we try to push through and make those C’s so we can still play.

Now, imagine

Dear [Principal's Name],

I believe it's unfair to prevent students with C averages from competing in sports. You might ask, "Why?" 

In my opinion, they should still be permitted to participate. I understand this firsthand since I've struggled with C's while excelling in sports like basketball and track. We put in a lot of effort just to achieve C's, and if the rule was stricter, fewer students might participate in athletics because it's challenging to earn a B or A.
